name: Babi Ketjap (Soy Pork)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dice the pork and season with salt and pepper.
2. Fry the pork in hot oil or margarine until browned.
3. Add finely sliced onions and cook until softened.
4. Stir in ground ginger, sambal oelek, and water.
5. Pour in sweet soy sauce and bring to a boil.
6. Simmer for at least 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pork is tender.
7. Garnish with sliced tomatoes and chopped vegetable pickles.
8. Serve with plain or savory rice or noodles.
Note: For best results, use one-inch pieces of baby back ribs or larger chunks of pork to prevent drying out. If sambal oelek is unavailable, substitute with a similar hot condiment mixed with lime juice.
